item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Could be an AWCY?, but ultimately works better as not. Neat to see another SCP that isn't AWCY? artifying the world with its statements going so far.

There's a lot of them around now that aren't, but this one makes me feel for some reason.

Take your +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm this seems interestin…

FUCK THE FOUNDATION

YES +>9000!!!!!!12

Footnotes
1. OVER 9000 UPBOATS
2. Don't get me wrong, I like this website, but the Foundation in-universe can be some sons of bitches sometimes. >:P This is why I'm more of a Serpent's Hand/REFORM THE FUCKING ETHICS COMMITTEE kinda guy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We already have lots of living drawings/graffiti, and lots of SCPs being aware of the Foundation and trying to break containment. That being said, I found this good enough to give a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this is kind of bland. I like the way it gets signs, but we've already got living graffiti that can transfer itself to other surfaces.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weird, wacky and original, I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's very competently written. It's just not interesting, in my opinion. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Synthetic-Organic Marriage Equality lobby won't like that…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you're not based off of carbon but are sentient, you can't get married?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, sorry about that. Part of me knew I wasn't going to be able to get my full thoughts in fast enough but that's what I get for hitting enter so fast.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If it's what you're referencing, I'd pay good money for a Nero's Day at Disneyland Scip.

Edit: Now that I saw your edit… Seems interesting, but I think that'd be more suited towards Tale material. Which I might write one day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It needs a gas mask with Mickey Mouse ears that is bleeding dollar bills onto a starving immigrant Bart Simpson.

Edit: That sounds flippant, I should explain. This SCP is neither insane enough or realistic enough to be good. Right now it just reads like a little bland wish fulfillment with a too mild twist of "it got people killed" at the end.

This thing should both misunderstand what it is doing and turn it up to 11. It needs to embarrass Banksy in its overreach, and then it needs to go on a purity quest where it turns the people it superficially supports against each other by constantly one-upping whatever position they hold with a bigger word stew including terms like "colonialism" "patriarchy" "cisgendernormativity" and such that make the average Tumblr bio seem tame by comparison.

When it is at a minimum wage rally it needs to demand something higher than what the protestors are asking for. If there's a marriage equality rally it demands polygamy. Its motivation should be to be more better and more authentic and more opporessed than anyone around it. And if anyone near it questions its message or advocates for restraint it needs to rally everyone around to destroy the white racist bigoted xtian from Texas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Those numbers are exactly what I'd want/expect from a mind-affecting SCP. Like I complain about a lot, the thing I hate most is when SCPs go the route of "affecting everyone's minds equally" because people aren't all the same, mentally, and theoretically a mind trick that could turn someone's opinion one way could have another person develop an irrational hatred for something completely different.

Also

SCP-2866 produced a sign that read "FUCK THE FOUNDATION."

Ey, fuck you buddy >=O

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it in chat, like it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good point about the same-sex marriage legislation; I didn't consider that. Thanks for the upvote though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the article is very well written.

Personally, I don't really like the idea that same-sex marriage legislation was the result of a SCP. I think it kind of undermines the huge win that it was. Also, I think the Occupy Wall Street reference will quickly become dated, but I may be wrong.

Other than that, good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
33/62 (~53%) NY State Senators voted in favor of the bill; a 2011 public opinion found that 58% of NY voters were in favor of same-sex marriage. Those numbers are slightly lower than what I'd personally think a mind-affecting SCP could achieve. If the blackboxes break anyone else's suspension of disbelief, I'll omit them and just be a little bit more general.

Thank you for the upvote though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know what. I like this. It's not overly dramatic, it doesn't try to be something overwhelming, the influence is subtle, and it's well written. All of these things are plenty to warrant a +1 from me.

A minor pick, though.

Same-sex marriage legislation was voted upon in New York seven days after the incident. ██% of people voted in favor of its legalization, compared to only █% negative votes.

No real need to blackbox that, since that was a real event and the numbers are public.
